--- USER-FRIENDLY OUTPUT ---
📋 DIAGNOSIS:
• Raktagatavata (Vata aggravated in blood)

📖 EXPLANATION:
• Sharp pain in the leg and warmth to the touch are indicative of Vata aggravation (ayurvedic_treatment_file1).
• Swelling in the legs is a symptom associated with Raktagatavata as described in Ayurvedic texts (24052018_CCRAS_Cardiac_disorders).
• Chest discomfort alongside leg symptoms suggests Vata moving in the blood channels (24052018_CCRAS_Cardiac_disorders).
• Raktagatavata involves multiple joints with severe pain and swelling (ayurvedic_treatment_file1).
• Conditions like Rheumatoid Arthritis can present similarly to Raktagatavata (ayurvedic_treatment_file1).

⚠️ IS IT SERIOUS?
• Yes
• The presence of chest discomfort alongside leg swelling raises concern for potential cardiac involvement (24052018_CCRAS_Cardiac_disorders).
• Raktagatavata, if left untreated, can lead to chronic pain and disability (ayurvedic_treatment_file1).
• Cardiac disorders can be life-threatening and require prompt medical attention (24052018_CCRAS_Cardiac_disorders).

🏠 CAN IT BE TREATED AT HOME?
• No
• While Ayurvedic principles can support management, the combination of symptoms necessitates professional evaluation to rule out serious underlying conditions (24052018_CCRAS_Cardiac_disorders).
• Chest discomfort requires immediate medical assessment to determine the cause (24052018_CCRAS_Cardiac_disorders).
• Self-treating without a proper diagnosis could delay appropriate care and worsen the condition (ayurvedic_treatment_file1).
